publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-03-22 23:09:05 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Diaries, scrapbooks, manuscripts, printed materials, and correspondence.
did.abstract: Papers of George Frederick Wells include diaries; writings on various theological questions; sermons; scrapbooks and notebooks; pamphlets, correspondence, tracts and church programs.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Papers of George Frederick Wells include diaries, 1905-1958; writings on the rural church and various theological questions; sermons; scrapbooks and notebooks; and pamphlets, tracts and church programs. Also, correspondence, 1900-1958, much of it with World War II servicemen, but also concerning the Country Church Movement.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: George Frederick Wells papers, #2141. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: World War, 1939-1945 -- Methodists.
World War, 1939-1945 -- Paricipation, American.
Religion -- Study and teaching -- United States.
Methodist preaching.
Methodist Church (U.S.)
Methodists -- United States.
Rural churches.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Loose-leaf: Index to Reverend Wells' sermons by subject, also lists numbers, title, and text

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These papers were added to the folder and are not Well's. Church programs, book and magazine mail advertising, newspaper clippings, mostly columns by George Mathew Adams, Church pamphlets and tracts, two Pamphlets on "Atlantic Union", two pledge cards, calling card "Reverend Lincoln MacKenzie", membership card for G. F. W. for League of Vermont Writer, Valentine, Facsimile title page of Zion's Herald of 1823/01/09 - "The Federal Churches of Lincoln, Vermont. Articles of Amalgamation", reprint: "The Last Best Hope of Earth: An Interview with the Vice-President of the United State" by Frank S. Mead, Editor of the 'Christian Herald', "Appeal to Men in Service" by W. C. T. U., "The Ten Commandments of Safety", "Why I Go To Temple" by Samuel B. Finkel, and various scraps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A handful of receipts, 1 invoice, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sesquicentennial of Columbia Co., N.Y., Scouting, Boys in the Service to whom he wrote, Church unity, Country Life, Country Life Leaders, obituaries, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scouting (mostly), Red Cross, Ground Observers' Corps, Agricultural Missions Foundation, Methodist Rural Fellowship, etc.

unittitle.title: Report of Child and Family Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet. Ts.cc. "The Board of Sunday Schools Extension Department of Annual Report of the Eastern Division New York, New York East and Newark Conferences"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and clippings pertaining to the religious education of children, radio sermon on the same subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Misc. clippings, notes, programs, scratch paper, and transcript of radio talk.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly letters (some postcards and Christmas cards), correspondence with servicemen, their letters and frequently carbons of his replies, occasional clippings, church programs, or pictures. States he writes to about 60 men [probably more than that - J.K.] 1940-45 not in order, still in envelopes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters: Most faithful correspondent. James J. Hardie most literate among servicemen, along with Raymond Roberts. Occasional letters, to and from chaplains and ministers. 1941-44, not in order, still in envelopes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4" x 6" card file with the names of all military correspondents entered on them; 3" x 5" card file with names and addresses of all military correspondents. 1940-45, not in order. One set almost duplicates the other set exactly.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Carbon copies of Rev. Wells' letters to servicemen" The folder letters as mentioned clippings about the soldiers posted to loose-leaf paper. Not in order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper clippings from Windham Journal concerning churches, town history, and obituaries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mimeo of Wells' M. A. thesis, University of Vermont: The Social Problem of the Country Church

did.unittitle: Echoes From a Country Parish by Sandford Faulkner Boardman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mimeo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook: Clippings, notes, etc., concerning the league

unittitle.title: A Minister and His Predecessors Critique of Ministerial Ethics Letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ministerial Ethics - Ms of "A Minister and His Predecessors Critique of Ministerial Ethics" by Reverend Horace Hoffman. Letters between them in the year 1934.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Israel - letters of 1937, to and from and concerning Henry Israel of the National Council of the Y. M. C. A.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dawber - letters of 1937-38, to and from Reverend Mark A. Dawber of the Board of Home Missions and Church Extension of the Methodist Episcopal Church.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters of 1941 to and from various ministers for information on the Country Life Movement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Periodical. One issue

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pages with newspaper clippings related to the serviceman and religion, also many copies of prayers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Quarterly, April 1912

unittitle.title: The Church and Veterans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet: findings and speeches of Bishops' Conference with Returned Veterans, Columbus, Ohio, July 4-5-6-7, 1946.

unittitle.title: Women's Foreign Missionary Society Leader's Workbook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook (black): Carbons of letters to servicemen, 1942-1945; occasional clippings; and three issues of the "Messenger".

unittitle.title: My First 100 Sermons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Church Management. December 1917, and Ms. Of "My First 100 Sermons"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ms. of "The Rural Church and Social Engineering."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters (1903-06) to and from Joseph L. Hills, of University of Vermont concerning the publication of his senior thesis "Status of Rural Vermont" and to and from Kenyon L. Butterfield of Carnegie Institute concerning work Wells did for C. I.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper and religious journal clippings. 1898-1921.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sermons, articles, stray pages and notes.

unittitle.title: Report of Reverend G. Frederick Wells on the Correlation of Christian Forces

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript of The Unity, Federation, and A Report submitted in response to the request of the Business Committee of the Federal Council made by their Meeting held in New York, April 12, 1911.

unittitle.title: How to Build Up the Small Country Church. Submitted to the Homilectic Review

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several drafts

unittitle.title: Church Comity and Domestic Missions in Vermont

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ms. (several drafts of it)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ms. of "The Institutional Church in History" and Ms. of "The Literature of the Country Church Movement."

unittitle.title: 9,555 Parish Calls: A Preliminary Study

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ms.:

unittitle.title: 9,555 Parish Calls: A Preliminary Study

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black loose-leaf notebook: correspondence of 1912, 1917, 1921, 1935, 1939, 1941 and miscellaneous including, letters of recommendations for Reverend Wells, letters to ministers, etc., notes, newspaper clippings, medical forms, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook: clippings, notes, and programs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook: Daily Vacation Bible School; list of contributions, bills, programs, clippings, notes, etc. [2]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With picture of Scout pasted on the cover; notes on Scout troops, clippings, tellers, bills.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook: game instruction, articles and clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook (maroon): - notes on art, clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brown notebook: Sermon notes, articles, pamphlets.

unittitle.title: Wells, George Frederick, 'The Rural Church on Community Service'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [2 copies]

unittitle.title: Wells, George Frederick, Report of Daily Vacation Bible School at Windham

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [2 copies reprinted from Windham Journal]

unittitle.title: Church Comity Lecture Notes
A Select Bibliography of the Country Church
The Federated Churches of Lincoln, Vermont. Article of Amalgamation
Church Federation Facts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Numbers 1, 2, 3. [2 each]

unittitle.title: Christian Unity in Fact and Practice

did.unittitle: An Answer to the New England Church Question by George F. Wells
Vermont and the Rural Churches by George F. Wells

unittitle.title: Rural Exodus and Social Progress, 1903. Commencement Address
Rural Exodus and Social Progress, 1906. Unpublished Article

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Different from 1903. Rejection notice from Popular Science.

unittitle.title: Origin of the Country Church Movement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ms.

unittitle.title: Country Life Commission of California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: letter 15 January 1914 from Rufus R. Wilson. Humboldt Chamber of Commerce.

unittitle.title: A Social Survey for Rural Communities

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet by Wells, 1911.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter dated 6 November 1941 from W. A. Anderson, Cornell, returning manuscript.

unittitle.title: Old Home Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters and news clipping, regarding "Old Home Day" at various parishes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters 1915-50, clippings, notes and miscellaneous.

did.unittitle: Home Missions Council. Town and Country Committee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carbon of letter written 11 January 1938.

unittitle.title: Ralph Williamson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters to and from Ralph Williamson on the Rural Church Institute - 1951, 1953. The Rural Fellowship, Summer 1951.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters to and from Benson Y. Landis, The Home Missions Council 1937-38, programs, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mimeo notes on the subject.

unittitle.title: God's Rural Chores

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: CC. Ts. "God's Rural Chores" by Wells. Letters to and from Reverend John M. Pearson of the New York Conference, 1939-41 ; issue of Delaware County 4-H Club News; news release 20 April 1941; U.S.D.A. Extension Service Circular 351, March 1941.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, clippings, pamphlets, form letters 1936-37.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, form letters on rural church movement.

unittitle.title: Lord's Acre Plan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reprint issue of Farmer's Federation News, March 1946, pamphlet on above subject.

unittitle.title: Rural City of God

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on subject, church program. Letter to "Dear Oscar" [probably his brother] written on 24 August 1941.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters 1940-41 to Arthur, Oscar, and Lucy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of The Pastor's Journal, March 1935 "A Minister and His Predecessors" by Wells. Letter, 26 February, 1938, from Thomas Alfred Tripp, Board of Home Missions of Congregational Church. Clippings, church program, 3 letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Programs, form letters, announcements

unittitle.title: The New England Country Church Question

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mimeo Ts. By Wells

did.unittitle: W. L. Anderson and Professor Frank A. Waugh

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, biographical material on W. L. Anderson. Letters to and from Frank A. Waugh of Massachusetts A. C. 1937.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black. Clippings, notes, programs, an occasional letter mostly pertaining to history of local parishes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The issues of March 11, 18, 25, 1953 and of April 1, and 8, 1953. Clippings have been removed.

scopecontent.p: cc. Ts.:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: , papers, and clippings pertaining to James Weed, one of his servicemen correspondents.

scopecontent.p: Carbon paper box mostly letters to and from people who like his poetry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: and ms. Pages concerning Well's attempt to write a book for children. Clippings, too.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: pamphlets: (1) "The Size of the Rural Parish" by Ralph A. Fenton; (2) "The Salary of Rural Pastors" by Ralph A. Fenton; (3) "The Rural Church at Worship"; programs. Letters to and from ministers mostly, 1937, some 1941. Letter from Heber J. Grant, autograph of Mormon President; dated 1937.

unittitle.title: Retired Preachers. What a Retired Preacher Does

unittitle.title: 1803-1903, Centennial of the Old First Congregational Church, Windham, New York

unittitle.title: The History of the Old Church with Shorter Histories of the Daughter Churches of Windham, Jewett, and Ashland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet. 64 pp.

unittitle.title: The Book of Remembrance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: History of the Methodist Episcopal Church of Treadwell, New York. [1931?] 13 pages of text and pictures and 45 pages of advertisements at the end. Pulled, placed in Master file and catalogued separately, K. Jacklin, 4/27/65.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black scrapbook. Clippings pasted in [1907-1932] and notes primarily concerning weekday Bible School.

scopecontent.p: Ms.

scopecontent.p: Notes

unittitle.title: A Second Word to Ministerial Associations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ms.

unittitle.title: You Ought to Know

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ts. [2 copies]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scriptural quotations and clippings, poetry (original and otherwise) some obituary clippings, prayers - original and otherwise.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, rough drafts, questionnaires, mimeo bibliographies, price lists, and other material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers from his stint with the Ground Observer Corps, Albany Air Defense Region.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, odd pages, etc. probably from sermon preparation.

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings, programs, adverting pamphlets, other odd matter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper clippings, programs, advertising pamphlets, other odd printed matter.

did.unittitle: How One Country Pastor Did Community Work by George F. Wells

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ts. And Ms. Copies of Wells' poetry. Nothing on Federal Council.

unittitle.title: The Social Problem of the Country Church by Reverend Wells

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ts. 154 pp.

unittitle.title: Community Leadership: A Course in Social Engineering for Village and Country Communities Prepared for the State Executive Committee of the Young Men's Christian Association of New York 1914

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mimeo Ts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labelled in numbered envelopes. 74. "Not Forsaken God's Care of the Aged." 87. "Mountain Peaks." 88. "Mountain Peaks." 89. "Mountain Peaks." 91. "This Grand Display of God" 92. "The God of Elijah"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Other envelopes unnumbered: "'The Country Church,' Lecture Notes"; "What the First American Methodist Stood For"; "The Country Church and Social Services, The Gospel of the Kingdom"; "How to Organize a Church Federation"; "How Two Country Churches Became One"; "Inter-Church Federation"; "The Needs of the Country Church"; "The Mission of Methodism in New England"; "The Survey-Idea in Country-Life Work" by L.H. Bailey, a pamphlet

unittitle.title: Anniversary Oleout

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [placed in this empty folder by J. K.] scraps and fragment sermons, articles, etc. scratch paper- anything odd that does not form some recognizable whole.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: New York Daily News of June 12, 1939 and clippings of the U.S. Visit of King George and Queen Elizabeth and a few of Roosevelt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: References/recommendations for boys desiring to join Reverend Wells' Scout troop at Hyde Park. Ts. Cc. "A Challenge"; Ts. CC. "In God's Country"; Ts. CC. "A Minister' and His Predecessors"; Ts. CC. "The Minister's Message to the Rural People" [2 copies]; Ts. "Origin of the Country Church Movement"; Ms. "The Preachers in New York City"; Ms. "Nitrogen-really on nitrogen, not a sermon, apparently (dated 26, November, 1900) done as a paper at the University of Vermont; Ts. CC. "Why Not Christian Funeral"; Ts. CC. "Ministerial Ethics and the Christian Funeral"; Bibliography of the Country Church Movement; Ts. "Decent Christian Burial condensed form the Forum" by "Marian J. Caste"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters in envelopes. In chronological order by year - some undated

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters - flat filed (1900-1958). In chronological order by year - some undated. Four E. B. Sanford letters: July 15, September 18, 1922; February 12, 1923; February 26, 1931.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book. Editor, Elias B. Sanford. New York: Fleming H. Revell Company.

did.unittitle: The Country Church and the Rural Problem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book. Kenyon L. Butterfield. Chicago: University of Chicago Press.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book. Chicago: University of Chicago Press.

did.unittitle: The Church of the Open Country

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book. Warren H. Wilson. New York: Missionary Education Movement of the United State and Canada.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book. New York: Missionary Education Movement of the United States and Canada.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book. Anna B. Taft. New York: Missionary Education Movement of the United States and Canada.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book. George F. Wells. St. Alban's, Vermont: Vermont State Agricultural Commission, 1903.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book. James G. Blaine. Washington: Government Printing Office. Distributed 1965-01-07 per KJ

did.unittitle: Sanders' New Speller, Definer and Analyzer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book. Charles W. Sanders. New York and Chicago: Ivison, Blakeman, Taylor & Company. Distributed 1965-01-07 per KJ.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Periodicals. Saved for Rollin Lynde Hartt's article "A New England Hill Town", page 712.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Periodicals. Saved for Charles A. Brigg's "The Great Obstacle to the Reunion of Christendom", page 72.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Periodical. "The Country Church and its Social Problems" by Wells.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Periodical. the "New England Country Church Question" by Wells.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Periodical. "Writings on Practical Country Church Problems" by Wells.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Periodical. "Writings on Practical Country Church Problems" by Wells.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Country Church and the Making of Manhood" by Wells

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Social Misfortune of the Rescue Mission" by Wells

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "What Is Church Federation" by Wells

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "What Is Church Federation" by Wells

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "How to Federate and Unite Country Churches" by Wells [2 copies]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Periodical. "How to Build Up the Small Country Church" by Wells.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Periodical. July-August, 1907 - "What Our Country Churches Need" by Wells; November-December, 1912 - "The Rural Church in Community Service" by Wells; November-December, 1915 - "Efficient Church Union for Country Towns" by Wells.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (black). Clippings, programs, notes, etc., mainly related to Sunday School. Photograph pasted to back cover.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook (tan canvas). newspaper clippings, magazine articles, principally by Reverend Wells, programs related to Church Federation and comity.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook (tan canvas) binding. W 2 1909-1919, 1929. newspaper clippings, magazine article, principally by Reverend Well, programs, notes, one letter, chiefly related to Church Federation and comity.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Group photograph. Probably Cornell University Summer School for Rural Ministers. Also attendance list.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook. Picture pasted on cover, two men, one of which has Einstein-like hair. drafts of letters, notes, clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary. notes from 1 January, 1905 to 5 March, 1909. A comparative record for five years with all same dates on same page. Many gaps, very superficial.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary. notes from: 1 January, 1911 - 16 June, 1911; 17 May, 1913 - 20 May, 1913; 8 March, 1914 - 18 May, 1914; 6 April, 1917 - 31 December, 1917; 1 January, 1918 - 31 December, 1918; 1 January, 1919 - 31 December, 1919; 1 January, 1920 - 22 December, 1920; 1 January, 1925 - 31 December, 1925. Many gaps within these inclusive dates. Notes: 25 January 1911, meets President Taft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary. notes from 1 January, 1923 to 31 December, 1923. Many gaps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary. notes from 1 January, 1924 to 30 September, 1924. Mostly blank.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary. notes from 1 January, 1926 to 31 December, 1926. Quite faithful, snapshot pasted in back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary. notes from 1 January, 1927 to 31 December, 1927. Quite faithful.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary. notes from 1 January, 1928 to 31 December 1928.

scopecontent.p: Diary. notes from 1 January, 1929 to 31 December, 1929.
